bead.title: retry-power-policy: DDx-owned bead retry escalates requested power
bead.description:
<context-digest>DDx owns bead retry because DDx owns success classification evidence: commits, merge/preserve result, no-changes rationale, gates, review verdicts, cooldowns, and prior actual model/power. Agent owns model routing inside MinPower/MaxPower bounds and passthrough constraints.</context-digest>
Implement DDx retry policy that can raise MinPower after an eligible unsuccessful bead attempt without selecting or mutating a concrete model/provider. Deterministic setup/operator-action failures and passthrough exhaustion must stop instead of escalating.
bead.acceptance:
1. Retry policy reads prior attempt outcome and actual_power from run/try evidence.
2. On named retryable outcomes, the next try raises MinPower while preserving MaxPower.
3. Policy can request a top-model-only MinPower threshold derived from the agent model/power catalog.
4. Policy never emits DDx-selected Harness, Provider, Model, or ModelRef pins on normal execution.
5. Policy preserves any operator passthrough envelope unchanged across retries and does not inspect string values.
6. Tests cover success no-retry, no_changes_after_attempt retry escalation, review_blocked_capability retry escalation, passthrough-stickiness, max-power/no-progress stop behavior, and deterministic setup failures that do not retry.
7. Tests cover passthrough exhaustion stopping as blocked_by_passthrough_constraint or agent_power_unsatisfied without mutating pins or calling ResolveRoute.
8. Audit records include retry/stop classification reason and evidence.
